metaTitle: Image Compressor — Reduce File Size (Free, Private & No Quality Loss)
metaDescription: Compress JPG, PNG, and WebP images instantly in your browser. No file uploads required. 100% private — your photos never leave your device. Save storage for free.
introSummary: Our professional Image Compressor is built for speed and absolute privacy. Unlike traditional online optimizers that require you to upload sensitive photos to their servers, our tool processes everything locally on your device. Whether you need to shrink high-resolution JPGs for email, optimize PNGs for web performance, or convert images to the ultra-efficient WebP format, you can achieve up to 90% size reduction without visible quality loss.

contextUsage.title: For Web & SEO Performance
contextUsage.desc: Boost your website's Core Web Vitals by shrinking heavy images. Reduce LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) times with highly optimized WebP and JPG files.

contextUsage.title: For Email & Job Applications
contextUsage.desc: Easily meet strict file size limits (e.g., under 1MB or 200kb) for resume uploads, government forms, or email attachments.

contextUsage.title: For Mobile Storage Management
contextUsage.desc: Free up gigabytes of space on your smartphone by compressing bulky high-res photos before moving them to cloud storage or keeping them in your gallery.

howto.description: Click to select your JPG, PNG, or WebP files. Since we use local browser processing, even massive 20MB+ files load instantly without using your data plan.
Adjust the compression slider to balance file size and visual fidelity. Our real-time estimator shows you the potential 'New Size' before you even start.
Use our high-resolution preview to compare the original and compressed versions. Zoom in to ensure text and textures remain sharp and professional.
Click 'Compress & Download'. The optimized file is generated within your browser's memory and saved directly to your device.

why: Privacy-First Architecture — Your private documents and personal photos are never sent to any cloud or server. 100% local execution.
Intelligent Lossy/Lossless Logic — Removes hidden bloat and redundant metadata (EXIF) while keeping every essential pixel intact.
WebP Power Optimization — Convert standard PNGs to WebP during compression for industry-leading size reduction (up to 90%).
Zero Data Consumption — Perfect for travelers or those on limited data plans; you only use data once to load the initial page.
No Bulk Limits — Compress as many images as you need. No daily caps, no file size restrictions, and no hidden subscriptions.
Cross-Platform Ready — Works perfectly on Chrome, Safari, and Firefox, across iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS.

faq.q: How can I compress an image to exactly 200kb?
faq.a: Simply slide the quality bar while watching our 'Estimated Size' readout. Most smartphone photos can easily reach 200kb at around 70-80% quality settings.

faq.q: Will my images lose their sharpness?
faq.a: Our algorithm is tuned to preserve 'perceptual quality'. At 80% compression, the human eye typically cannot distinguish between the original and the optimized version.

faq.q: Why is 'No Upload' safer than other sites?
faq.a: Standard sites store your images on their servers for processing. Our tool uses 'Web Workers' to process the image inside your browser, meaning your data never touches the internet.

faq.q: Can I compress PNG files with transparency?
faq.a: Yes. Our compressor preserves alpha channels (transparency) in PNG and WebP formats, making it ideal for web logos and UI elements.

faq.q: Does this tool remove EXIF data (location, camera info)?
faq.a: By default, our high-compression mode strips metadata to save space and protect your privacy, ensuring your GPS location isn't hidden inside the file.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Desktop-class compression speed directly in the browser
Industry-standard compatibility for web, social media, and print
Secure handling of sensitive medical or legal documents
Handles large images without crashing mobile browsers
Maximum storage savings for mobile and cloud users

troubleshooting: File size increased? This rarely happens, but if an image is already extremely optimized, reapplying compression can occasionally add overhead. Try converting to WebP instead.
Images look blurry? Increase the quality slider to 85% or higher. For text-heavy documents, we recommend keeping the quality above 90%.
Slow processing on mobile? Very large images (5000px+) require significant CPU power. Close other browser tabs to give the compressor more resources.
proTips: For the absolute smallest file size with zero quality loss, always choose 'WebP' as your output format if your target platform supports it.
If you're uploading to a website like LinkedIn or a job portal, a quality setting of 75% is the 'sweet spot' for professional-looking images at tiny sizes.
Use this tool to strip hidden location data (GPS) from your photos before sharing them on public social media platforms for extra privacy.
